Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 96 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. LLM Architecture Gallery 231 upvotes, 18 comments
  3. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 314 upvotes, 29 comments
  4. Kangina 69 upvotes, 5 comments
  5. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 120 upvotes, 24 comments
  6. Nasdaq's Shame 130 upvotes, 27 comments
  7. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance of Canadians 287 upvotes, 74 comments
  8. Bandit: A 32bit baremetal computer that runs Color Forth [video] 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. A new Bigfoot documentary helps explain our conspiracy-minded era 37 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 58 upvotes, 13 comments
  11. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 105 upvotes, 31 comments
  13. Excel incorrectly assumes that the year 1900 is a leap year 41 upvotes, 10 comments
  14. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 112 upvotes, 41 comments
  15. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 118 upvotes, 44 comments
  16. Chrome DevTools MCP 317 upvotes, 141 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 217 upvotes, 94 comments
  18. I'm Too Lazy to Check Datadog Every Morning, So I Made AI Do It 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  19. Show HN: Open-source playground to red-team AI agents with exploits published 17 upvotes, 3 comments
  20. Cannabinoids remove plaque-forming Alzheimer's proteins from brain cells 40 upvotes, 14 comments
  21. The 49MB web page 286 upvotes, 158 comments
  22. Stop Sloppypasta 106 upvotes, 54 comments
  23. C++26: The Oxford Variadic Comma 114 upvotes, 63 comments
  24. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 220 upvotes, 135 comments
  25. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 174 upvotes, 115 comments
  26. Type systems are leaky abstractions: the case of Map.take!/2 19 upvotes, 7 comments
  27. Show HN: Free OpenAI API Access with ChatGPT Account 25 upvotes, 11 comments
  28. LLMs can be exhausting 70 upvotes, 56 comments
  29. Ask HN: How is AI-assisted coding going for you professionally? 238 upvotes, 395 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 122 upvotes, 394 comments

Last generated 9:30am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
